title: Sharing the Road
description: Bicycles, motorcycles, big trucks, and work zones.

scenarios.prompt: You are passing a person on a bicycle. How much room should you give?

choices.label: At least 3 feet of space
A few inches is fine
No extra room needed
About 1 foot
Honk and squeeze past

feedback.correct: Yes — give a bicycle at least 3 feet of space when you pass.
feedback.inches: That's too close. Give a bicycle at least 3 feet. Try again.
feedback.none: Bicycles need room — at least 3 feet when passing. Try again.
feedback.onefoot: One foot is too close. Give a bicycle at least 3 feet. Try again.
feedback.honk: Don't squeeze past — give a bicycle at least 3 feet of room. Try again.
feedback.default: Give a bicycle at least 3 feet of space. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: A big truck is next to you. Where is it most dangerous to drive?

choices.label: Right beside it, where the driver can't see you
Far in front, where it can see you
Well behind it with space

feedback.correct: Yes — trucks have big blind spots on the sides. If you can't see the driver's mirror, they can't see you.
feedback.far: Being well in front where the driver can see you is safer. The danger is right beside the truck. Try again.
feedback.behind: Staying back with space is safe. The danger zone is right alongside the truck. Try again.
feedback.default: The danger is beside the truck, in its blind spot. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: A big truck ahead is turning right. It swings out wide. What should you do?

choices.label: Wait behind it — never squeeze in on its right
Slip past on the right by the curb
Honk so it turns faster
Speed up and pass before it turns

feedback.correct: Yes — trucks swing wide to turn right. Stay back. Never squeeze between the truck and the curb.
feedback.squeeze: That spot is very dangerous. The truck can turn into you. Wait behind it. Try again.
feedback.honk: Honking won't help. Just wait behind the truck and give it room. Try again.
feedback.beat: Don't try to beat a turning truck — wait behind it. Try again.
feedback.default: Wait behind the truck. Don't squeeze in on its right side. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You just passed a big truck. When is it safe to move back in front of it?

choices.label: When you can see the whole truck in your mirror
Right away, as soon as you pass
After you tap your brakes to warn it
As soon as your back bumper is past it

feedback.correct: Yes — trucks need a lot of room to stop. Move over only when you can see the whole truck in your mirror.
feedback.rightaway: That's too soon. Trucks can't stop fast. Wait until you see the whole truck in your mirror. Try again.
feedback.tap: Never tap your brakes in front of a truck — it can't stop quickly. Pull ahead first. Try again.
feedback.bumper: That's still too close. Wait until you can see the whole truck in your mirror. Try again.
feedback.default: Wait until you can see the whole truck in your mirror before moving over. Try again.

scenarios.concept: Don't follow a truck too close
scenarios.prompt: You are driving behind a big truck. How much space should you leave?

choices.label: Extra space, so you can see and stop in time
Stay close so no one cuts in
The same space as behind a small car
Tuck in close behind to save gas

feedback.correct: Yes — leave extra space behind a truck. You can't see ahead of it, and the driver can't see you when you're close.
feedback.close: Too close is risky. You can't see the road ahead, and the truck can't see you. Back off. Try again.
feedback.same: A truck needs more space than a car. You can't see past it. Leave extra room. Try again.
feedback.draft: Tucking in close is dangerous — you can't see ahead and the driver can't see you. Leave extra space. Try again.
feedback.default: Leave extra space behind a big truck. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You see orange signs and workers ahead. What should you do?

choices.label: Slow down and watch for workers
Keep the same speed
Speed up to get through
Change lanes a lot to get ahead
Honk at the workers

feedback.correct: Yes — slow down in work zones and watch for people. Fines are often higher here too.
feedback.same: Slow down in a work zone — people are working close to the road. Try again.
feedback.fast: Never speed up in a work zone. Slow down and stay alert. Try again.
feedback.weave: Don't weave between lanes in a work zone — slow down and stay in your lane. Try again.
feedback.honk: Honking doesn't help — slow down and watch for workers. Try again.
feedback.default: Slow down and watch for workers. Try again.
